title: sedum telephium subsp. maximum
intro: Sedum telephium subsp. maximum, also known as the tall purple stonecrop, is a perennial succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Sedum telephium subsp. maximum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to the skin for its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
